mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample R-squared dropping below 0.01 over a 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden CPIAUCSL monthly increase above 0.4% triggers inflation fears and expectations of aggressive Fed tightening, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than half of triggered periods, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which flows through to corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.015 over a 36-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during an economic slowdown signals immediate supply chain contraction and earnings downgrade risk, forcing rapid S&P 500 depreciation within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 65% of severe INDPRO decline instances, or the trigger condition fails to separate market outcomes with statistical significance.
